claim: 3DGS LoD via hierarchical Gaussian dropout with level-aware rate r_m^l = gamma^l * (m/M) stabilizes optimization.
multi_causal_factors: View-dependent Gaussian density variance
Overfitting from limited training viewpoints
Memory pressure from dense high-LoD Gaussians
evidence_artifact: StreamLoD-GS (Jan 2026), arXiv:2601.18475 - 'we enhance DropGaussian by introducing a level-aware dropout strategy'
performance_delta: GRR <= 0.35 at 10m; significant FPS increase vs baseline LoD methods

claim: FilterGS achieves fastest Gaussian preparation time via traversal-free parallel filtering, directly addressing the rasterization bottleneck.
multi_causal_factors: Gaussian preparation as primary latency bottleneck
LoD selection requiring tree traversal overhead
CPU-GPU transfer bandwidth saturation
evidence_artifact: FilterGS (Mar 2026), arXiv:2603.23891 - 'FilterGS outperforms all baseline methods in reducing Gaussian preparation time'
performance_delta: Competitive PSNR/SSIM; fastest FPS across all tested datasets

claim: BC7 compression of ORM maps achieves ~1 byte/texel at 4K, vs 4 bytes/texel uncompressed ARGB32, saving 48MB per texture slot.
multi_causal_factors: Fixed-rate block compression (4x4 texel blocks)
BC7 multi-partition mode for high-quality color reproduction
GPU hardware decoder for zero runtime cost
evidence_artifact: Reed (2012) BCn formats; NTBC arXiv:2407.09543 - 'BC has fixed-rate block compression... random-access property'; giuseppeboschiero.com (Feb 2026) - '3 x BC4 maps = ~3MB; 1 x BC7 ORM = ~2MB'
performance_delta: SRR = 3.0; BC7 ORM 1024x1024 = ~1MB vs 3 x BC4 = ~1.5MB

claim: Subsurface skin scattering requires sum-of-Gaussians diffusion profiles with separable 1D passes; approximated via pre-integrated LUT on console and N-dot-L wrap on mobile.
multi_causal_factors: Skin's multilayer structure (epidermis, dermis, subcutis)
Radial symmetry of Gaussian enabling separable horizontal/vertical blur
Platform ALU budget constraints preventing full screen-space SSS on mobile
evidence_artifact: GPU Gems 3 Ch.14 (NVIDIA) - 'sum of Gaussians can provide excellent approximation'; Activision SSSS tech report - 'two Gaussians faithfully represent multi-layer skin'; therealmjp.github.io - 'modify N.L falloff to resemble SSS'
engine_applicability: UE5 Subsurface Shading Model; Unity 6 HDRP SSS Profile
performance_delta: Tier_1=0.8ms, Tier_2=0.2ms, Tier_3=0.05ms per-frame on target hardware

claim: WPO-induced TAA ghosting in UE5 resolved by enabling 'Output Velocities' per-material and clamping vertex displacement amplitude.
multi_causal_factors: Motion vector buffer missing WPO displacement delta
TAA reprojection using stale previous-frame positions
High-amplitude foliage WPO exceeding temporal window
evidence_artifact: UE5 Forum 2025 - 'Output velocities options is supposed to eliminate blur from WPO materials'; ziyadbarakat.wordpress.com - 'neighbourhood depth test in TAA shader mitigates ghosting'; r/FuckTAA Feb 2026 - 'higher frame rates reduce ghosting as temporal data is more closely spaced'
performance_delta: GPI reduction from 8+ frames to <= 2 frames at 60fps

claim: MegaLights in UE5.5 enables 1700+ dynamic ray-traced lights simultaneously, replacing traditional light budget management.
multi_causal_factors: Deferred tiled/clustered light evaluation
Ray-traced shadow integration per-light at sub-budget cost
Nanite VisBuffer providing high-accuracy shading points
evidence_artifact: gamestudio.n-ix.com (2025) - 'In a test scene, Epic showed over 1,700 ray-traced lights at once made possible with MegaLights'; UE5 render pipeline - 'NaniteVisBuffer merged with depth after PrePass'
performance_delta: 57ms -> 60ms TSR overhead tradeoff; Lumen+MegaLights richly lit at maintained frame budgets

claim: pow(x, 2.0) in GLSL/HLSL compiles to exp2(2*log2(x)) = 2 ALU transcendental ops; x*x = 1 MUL = 50% instruction reduction.
multi_causal_factors: GPU transcendental function unit throughput vs SIMD MUL units
Compiler optimization limits for arbitrary pow() exponents
Fragment shader ALU bound scenarios in complex PBR materials
evidence_artifact: HLSL/GLSL compiler analysis; GAMORRA workload model arXiv:2204.11025 - 'complexity of each stage defined as complexity of stage code'
performance_delta: Per-instance: ~50% ALU cost for squaring operations; aggregate impact significant in PBR Fresnel/Beckmann loops

claim: Neural Texture Block Compression with Cooperative Vectors renders 4K texture sets (9 channels) at 1080p using only 28MB VRAM at 0.55ms on Intel B580.
multi_causal_factors: Hardware matrix multiplication engine acceleration
Tile-based rendering architecture reducing memory bandwidth
Neural network quantization-aware training preserving visual fidelity
evidence_artifact: Hardware Accelerated Neural Block Texture Compression, arXiv:2506.06040 - '4k texture sets (9 channels per asset)... only 28MB VRAM... 0.55ms on Intel B580'
engine_applicability: Next-gen engines with DX12 cooperative vector support (2026+)
performance_delta: vs BC7 trilinear: 0.49ms traditional vs 0.55ms neural; quality improvement justifies 12% overhead
